The prophet must shave his head and beard, which signifies God’s complete rejection and abandonment of that people. One part must be burned in the middle of the city, showing the many who will perish by famine and disease. Another part was to be cut into pieces, representing those who were slain by the sword. Another part was to be scattered in the wind, showing how some are carried away to the land of the conqueror, and others flee to neighboring countries for safety. A small amount of the third portion was to be bound in his shirts, as something he is very careful about. Few were reserved. Wherever sinners flee, the fire and sword of God’s wrath will consume them.

The sentence passed upon Jerusalem is very terrible, and the way it is expressed makes it even more so. Who is able to stand in God's sight when he is angry? Those who live and die without repentance will perish forever unpitied; there is a day coming when the Lord will not spare anyone. Let those people or churches who change the Lord’s laws not expect to escape Jerusalem’s doom. Let us strive to honor the teaching of God our Savior in all things. Sooner or later, God's word will prove itself true.
